title: VerusPanel
tagline: Open-source cPanel alternative for modern developers
description: An open-source server control panel that aims to be a modern alternative to cPanel. Built with a clean interface, flexible architecture, and a module system so developers can shape it to their own needs. Currently in pre-release.
longDescription:
VerusPanel is a self-hosted server management panel designed for developers who want full control without licensing costs. The project focuses on three pillars: a clean modern UI, a flexible module/plugin system, and zero vendor lock-in.

The first release is shipping with core service management (web server, PHP, databases, SSL), user/permission management, and a module SDK so third parties can extend functionality. Built primarily with PHP on the backend and a modern JavaScript frontend.
highlights: cPanel alternative, fully open source (MIT-style license)
Modern UI, no legacy Web-1.0 look
Targets developers, not just hosting resellers

title: RouteZero
tagline: AI model router & orchestrator with smart fallback chains
description: An AI model router similar to OpenCode Zen: define a fallback chain of LLM providers, RouteZero routes requests intelligently and falls back automatically when a model fails, hits rate limits, or goes over budget. Token budget tracking included.
longDescription:
RouteZero solves a real problem in production AI applications: model reliability and cost. Different providers have different strengths, prices, and uptime characteristics. Hard-coding one model is fragile; manually switching is tedious.

The platform provides a unified interface to 16+ LLM providers (Qwen, DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, MiniMax, and others), a drag-to-reorder fallback chain, per-model token pricing, a 190M-token budget tracker, and analytics. Supports Playground, Models, Keys, Status, Fallback, Analytics, and Batch Test panels out of the box.
highlights: 16+ LLM providers through a single API
Drag-to-reorder fallback chain — never get stuck on a failed model
Per-model token pricing & real-time budget tracking

title: VerusDB
tagline: Secure embedded database designed for developers
description: An embedded database built with developers in mind. No complex setup, no separate database server — install and start building. Designed for applications that need a reliable, self-contained data layer.
longDescription:
VerusDB is positioned as a developer-first embedded database: drop it into your project, no server to spin up, no separate process to manage. The focus is on a small, predictable surface area and a clean API.

The project includes a secure-by-default configuration, simple installation, and documentation that assumes you've got better things to do than read 80 pages before your first query.
highlights: Zero-setup embedded database — no separate server
Secure defaults out of the box
Self-contained: ships as a library, not a service
Designed for small-to-medium applications and tooling

title: SmartHesap
tagline: Cloud-based accounting & invoicing for small businesses
description: A SaaS application that helps small businesses issue invoices, track income/expenses, and calculate taxes — all in the cloud, fast and smart. MVP completed and announced on LinkedIn.
longDescription:
SmartHesap targets the long tail of small businesses in Turkey that still do their accounting on paper or in spreadsheets. The MVP includes invoice creation, income/expense tracking, and tax calculation flows designed for non-accountants.

Built as a SaaS from day one: cloud-hosted, multi-tenant, designed to scale. The product is currently in MVP stage and the next iteration focuses on bank integration and automated bookkeeping.
highlights: MVP shipped — invoice, income/expense, tax calc

title: Merbil Eğitim Araçları
tagline: E-commerce website for an educational tools retailer
description: A real client project: a website for Merbil Eğitim Araçları, a retailer of educational equipment (trampolines, sensory rooms, balance equipment). Built to convert visitors into WhatsApp conversations rather than act as a typical catalog.
longDescription:
Unlike a standard e-commerce site, the goal here was contact-first: drive visitors to WhatsApp rather than expecting them to checkout through a cart. The product cards are designed to highlight key benefits and end with a clear "WhatsApp'tan İletişim" call-to-action.

The site includes a product catalog with category filters, a contact form, About and Trust sections, and a mobile-first responsive design. After a two-week observation period post-launch, no critical issues surfaced.
highlights: Real client work — shipped and observed for 2 weeks
Trust and benefits sections to reduce friction

title: AI Image Detection
tagline: Deep learning image classification with CNNs
description: An image classification system built with TensorFlow and Keras. Custom CNN architecture trained on CIFAR-10 and custom datasets, with transfer learning experiments using VGG16 and ResNet50.
longDescription:
A practical dive into deep learning for computer vision. Built a custom CNN from scratch, then compared it against fine-tuned versions of VGG16 and ResNet50. Applied data augmentation, batch normalization, dropout, and learning rate scheduling to squeeze out accuracy.

The project includes the full training pipeline, evaluation on held-out test sets, and export of the trained model for inference. Final accuracy on the custom dataset reached 92% with top-5 at 98%.
highlights: Transfer learning: VGG16 and ResNet50 fine-tuning

title: CarParking Fuzzy
tagline: Fuzzy logic system for predicting parking availability
description: A Mamdani-type fuzzy inference system that predicts parking lot occupancy. Takes weather, time, distance, and event density as inputs and outputs a probability of finding a spot.
longDescription:
An experiment in using fuzzy logic for a real-world prediction problem. Crisp inputs are mapped to linguistic variables (e.g. 'weather is bad', 'rush hour'), rules are evaluated against a knowledge base, and the system outputs a defuzzified score.

Includes a visual rule editor, real-time calculation, and a detailed report explaining each rule and the reasoning behind it. The system is a decision-support tool rather than a black-box ML model, so its decisions are explainable.
highlights: Visual rule editor and live output
Explainable decisions — every output is traceable to rules
Detailed report on system design and validation

title: Customer Churn Analysis
tagline: End-to-end ML pipeline for predicting customer churn
description: An end-to-end data science project: data cleaning, feature engineering, training (Random Forest, Logistic Regression, XGBoost), hyperparameter tuning, and evaluation on a real telco dataset.
longDescription:
Churn prediction is the canonical classification problem for product teams. This project walks the full path: explore the data, clean it, engineer features, train multiple models, tune them, and pick the best one based on the right business metric.

EDA revealed which features actually matter, correlation analysis drove feature selection, and the final XGBoost model hit 89% accuracy with strong precision/recall. The repo includes a written report and reproducible notebooks.
highlights: Three algorithms compared: RF, LR, XGBoost

title: EyeDisaster
description: A web-based interface that uses the camera to track eye gaze and convert it into actions. Built for emergency scenarios and accessibility — hands-free control of critical UI elements.
longDescription:
In disaster scenarios or for users with motor impairments, traditional input devices aren't an option. EyeDisaster uses WebGazer.js to track gaze through a regular webcam, then maps dwell-time (looking at a target for X seconds) to clicks.

Includes a calibration flow for personal accuracy, an emergency UI with large action buttons, an SOS signal feature, and geolocation sharing. The whole thing runs in the browser — no native app required.
highlights: Emergency UI with large, clear actions
